paper_title: Paleopathology and Morphological Analysis of Canis chihliensis and Canis dirus

methods.description: This method involves the examination of the physical form and structure of the specimens to identify and document morphological features and any pathological conditions.

steps.description: Collect specimens from the Early Pleistocene site at Shanshenmiaozui (SSMZ) in the Nihewan Basin.
Examine the specimens for morphological features and pathological conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: examine specimens for morphology and pathology

methods.description: This method uses CT scanning and X-ray examination to analyze the internal structures of the specimens, providing detailed images for further analysis.

steps.description: Perform CT scanning on specimens following the slicing intervals of Rothschild, Wang & Shoshani (1994).
Conduct X-ray examination to assess internal structures.

methods.description: This method determines the age of the specimens using epiphyseal fusion and tooth wear analysis.

steps.description: Determine age based on epiphyseal fusion following Sumner-Smith (1966).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze epiphyseal fusion for age determination

steps.description: Assess tooth wear for age determination following Gipson, Ballard & Mech (2000).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze tooth wear for age determination

methods.description: This method estimates the body mass of the specimens using regression analysis on femur shaft diameter and m1 length.

steps.description: Calculate body-mass estimates using regressions on canid femur shaft diameter by Anyonge & Roman (2006).
steps.searchable_summary: perform regression analysis on femur shaft diameter for body-mass estimation

steps.description: Calculate body-mass estimates using m1 length by Van Valkenburgh (1990).
steps.searchable_summary: perform regression analysis on m1 length for body-mass estimation
